questions: You mention that agentic RAG only improved quality by 0.3 points on average but cost 66% more in tokens — why did you decide to keep building with agents instead of sticking with standard RAG?

You note that the complex strategic question (where you expected agents to shine most) ended up being a tie despite 5 searches. What do you think the agent was actually searching for on those extra 4 searches, and does that change how you think about the architecture?

You compare ChromaDB to SQLite and Pinecone to Postgres, but then joke about whether Anthropic gets a kickback from recommending Pinecone — did Claude's recommendation influence your choice, or would you have picked Pinecone anyway based on your own evaluation?
